executive_summary: This session shows strong room presence and distributed engagement. Consider refining gesture size to match emphasis and softening facial tone. Also, practice turning back to the audience more promptly after board or note checks to maintain connection.

priority_actions.title: Tighten the peak size of gestures
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Large bursts of motion can distract from the teaching point when they are not tightly timed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required. 00:15-00:30 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Keep big gestures for true emphasis and use smaller controlled hand movements elsewhere.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Soften the visible facial tone
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:15-00:30 showed a flatter or tighter visible facial tone than the rest of the clip.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether the face resets between points instead of staying tight through the whole sentence.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Turn back toward the audience sooner
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:00-00:15 spent longer than needed turned away from the audience after checks.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether board or note checks end with a quick shoulder-and-chin reset back to the room.
